title: polygala telephium
intro: Polygala telephium, also known as the "common or tall valerian" but more commonly referred to as 'Valerian', is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia. Its roots and rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Polygala telephium include promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. The herbal preparations of Polygala telephium typically involve drying and processing the roots to be used as teas, tinctures, or capsules, which are available in various health food stores and online platforms.
